title: Second Conditional — Hypothetical Situations
description: Talk about unreal or unlikely present and future situations using if + past simple + would.

items.example: If I won the lottery, I would travel the world.

items.back: If I were you, … (not 'was' in formal English)
items.example: If she were here, she would know what to do.

items.front: If I were you, I would… (advice)
items.back: the classic way to give advice with second conditional
items.example: If I were you, I'd talk to her directly.

items.back: If she practised more, she could become a professional.
items.example: If we left now, we might catch the last train.

items.back: I wish I had more time. / She wishes she spoke better English.
items.example: I wish I lived closer to work — the commute is exhausting.

items.back: I would / she would / they would
items.example: I'd love to help if I could. They'd come if they were free.
If it rains, I'll stay. (likely) / If I were a bird, I'd fly to you. (impossible)

items.front: Would NOT use 'would' in the if-clause
items.back: If I would have → WRONG. If I had → CORRECT.
items.example: If I had a car, I'd give you a lift. (NOT: if I would have a car)

items.prompt: If I ___ a million dollars, I'd donate half to charity.
items.explanation: Second conditional: If + past simple. 'If I had a million dollars…' (imaginary).

items.prompt: If I ___ you, I would apologise immediately.
items.explanation: 'If I were you…' is the standard phrasing. 'Were' is used for all persons in second conditional.

items.prompt: She ___ the job if she spoke better English. Unfortunately she doesn't.
items.explanation: Unlikely present situation → second conditional: 'would get'. She doesn't speak good enough English.

items.prompt: I wish I ___ more time. There's so much I want to do.
items.explanation: 'Wish + past simple' expresses an unreal present wish: I wish I had. (but I don't)

items.prompt: If we left now, we ___ catch the last bus.
items.explanation: 'Might' in a second conditional = possible but not certain result: we might catch it.

items.sentence: If I ___ a superpower, I'd choose the ability to fly. (have)
If I were you, I ___ tell her the truth as soon as possible.
I wish I ___ closer to work — the commute takes two hours. (live)
She ___ buy a flat if she had enough savings. (could)
If he ___ harder, he'd get much better results. (work)
